MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Randall Wilkins

RULING:
DEFENDANT LIMBACH FACILITY SERVICES LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Limbach Facility Services, LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-2 (exposure/causation) and off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (strict liability and punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiff's claims against it. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff's claims are barred by the Privette doctrine. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty to plaintiff just because the asbestos-containing products and/or materials that its employees used, disturbed and/or installed in plaintiff's presence were not manufactured or installed by defendant (fireproofing) or were provided by plaintiff's employer (insulation inserts). Plaintiff has dismissed his strict liability cause of action and claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Jennifer Seelenbacher of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11878 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Brian R. Tinkham of POOLE & SHAFFERY, LLP (213-316-1504), appearing for defendant / moving party H & C Investment Associates, Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party / opposing party Randall Wilkins

RULING:
DEFENDANT H & C INVESTMENT ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant H&C Investment Associates, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is off calendar. The motion for summary judgment is denied because defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that any exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was not a substantial factor in causing or contributing to the development of plaintiff's asbestos-related disease. The declaration of Mr. Scoby is inadmissible because he demonstrates no foundation to state that any asbestos in a Childers product would have been encapsulated. The declarations of Dr. Rabinovitz and Dr. Sandler do not negate an element of plaintiff's claims because those declarations are largely inadmissible and assume, without foundation and based on Mr. Scoby's inadmissible declaration, that any asbestos that would have been released from plaintiff's removal, rasping and/or disturbance of the Childers products would have been encapsulated. The court considered plaintiff's written objections and, further, nothing prohibits plaintiff from making oral objections at the hearing. Plaintiff's objections to the declarations of Mr. Scoby, Dr. Rabinovitz and Dr. Sandler and to defendant's responses to standard interrogatories are sustained. Plaintiff has dismissed his claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Jennifer Seelenbacher of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11878 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
